description: Figure 6 presents a parity plot comparing machine-learning-predicted solid solution strengthening increments (Δσ_SSS, in MPa) against experimentally measured values for Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel under ten-fold cross-validation. Each scatter point represents an individual alloy sample, with the color scale on the right indicating the absolute prediction error ranging from 0.60 to 29.90 MPa. A dashed blue guide line marks the ideal one-to-one correspondence between predicted and experimental data. The plot quantifies model accuracy with an R² of 0.89 and an RMSE of 0.31. According to the caption, the predictive model uses {δ_Ec, D, G} as input features. The samples predominantly span the 1300–1700 MPa range and distribute tightly along the guide line, visually confirming that the coupled SVR and modified solid-solution-strengthening theory accurately captures the solid-solution hardening contribution across the investigated alloy compositions.
